entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
entries.learning_activity: Describe the concepts of relations and functions (linear and quadratic) (types of relations, domain and range of relations, graphs of relations and functions, inverse of relations, and functions)
teaching_learning_activities: Collaborative learning: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the concepts of relations, functions, types of relations and functions, and link between relations and functions
Exploration: Organise students in manageable groups to explore the concept of relations of family members and price of different commodities in different businesses
Visual representation: Guide students to use graphs, charts, and tables to discuss mappings of two sets
Project: In small groups, guide students to visit nearby shops and relate prices of different commodities with the concept of domain and range of functions
entries.assessment: Concepts of relations and functions are clearly described
resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
entries.learning_activity: Find the domain and range of relations and functions
teaching_learning_activities: Jigsaw: Organise students in manageable groups and guide them to determine domain and range from graphs, charts, and tables of relations and functions
entries.assessment: Domain and range of relations and functions are clearly described
resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
entries.learning_activity: Find the inverses of relations and functions
teaching_learning_activities: Problem solving: Equip students with appropriate materials on relations and functions. Guide them to discuss the provided questions on inverses of relations and functions and present their findings
entries.assessment: The inverse of relations and functions are correctly found
resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
entries.learning_activity: Draw graphs of relations and functions
teaching_learning_activities: Visual representation: In manageable groups guide students to use visual aid to draw graphs of relations and functions
Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as graphing calculators, and software such as MATLAB to draw graphs of relations and functions
entries.assessment: The graphs of relations and functions are correctly drawn
resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.assessment: The graphs of relations and functions are correctly drawn

entries.learning_activity: Draw graphs of relations and functions

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of algebra (linear programming: constraints, objective functions, and optimal solution)
teaching_learning_activities: Jigsaw: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the concepts of linear programming, constraints, objective functions, and optimal solutions of linear programming using real-life examples
Graphical methods: In manageable groups, guide students to use graphs to determine optimal solutions of linear programming problems with two variables
Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as Excel and MATLAB to determine the optimal solutions of linear programming problems with two variables
Problems solving: In manageable groups, guide students to find optimal solutions of linear programming problems
entries.assessment: The concepts of linear programming are clearly explored
resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as Excel and MATLAB to determine the optimal solutions of linear programming problems with two variables
Problems solving: In manageable groups, guide students to find optimal solutions of linear programming problems
entries.assessment: The concepts of linear programming are clearly explored
resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of sequences and series (Arithmetic progression AP , Geometric progression GP)
teaching_learning_activities: Exploration: Guide students to explore the concepts of sequences and series
Visual representation: Guide students to use visual aids such as charts, diagrams, graphs, and real objects to discuss the concepts of Arithmetic Progression (AP) and Geometric Progression (GP)
entries.assessment: The concepts of sequences and series are clearly explored
resources: sequence and series games and applications
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: Find the general term for AP and GP and use them to derive formulae for the sums of APs and GPs
teaching_learning_activities: Exploration: Organise students in manageable groups to discover and derive the nth terms of AP and GP
Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide students to determine the nth terms of APs and GPs
entries.assessment: The formulae for the sum of APs and GPs are correctly derived
resources: sequence and series games and applications
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: Calculate arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and compound interest
teaching_learning_activities: Exploration: Organise students in manageable groups to explore the concepts of arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and compound interest
Think-ink-pair-share: In manageable groups, instruct students to discuss and apply the concepts of arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and compound interest in their real-life situation
Interactive discussion: Guide students to apply the concept of compound interest in real-life situations, such as calculating the total cost of loan or the return on an investment over time
entries.assessment: The arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and compound interest are correctly found
resources: sequence and series games and applications
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

Interactive discussion: Guide students to apply the concept of compound interest in real-life situations, such as calculating the total cost of loan or the return on an investment over time
entries.assessment: The arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and compound interest are correctly found
resources: sequence and series games and applications
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
entries.specific_competence: Use basic skills of circles in daily life
entries.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of a circle (angle properties, theorems, tangents, chords and radians)
teaching_learning_activities: Think- ink-pair-share: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the concepts of angle properties of a circle, tangent, theorems, chords, and radians
Scenario: Provide a scenario that shows the application of circles. Allow learners to discuss, make follow up and provide feedback
Visual representation: Guide students to use visual aids such as diagrams, pictures, and animations to understand the concepts of angle properties of a circle, tangent, theorems, chords, and radians
entries.assessment: The concept of circle is clearly explored
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.assessment: The concept of circle is clearly explored
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education
